title: The Barefooted Friar
content:
1.

آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll give thee, good fellow, a twelvemonth or twain,
آ آ آ آ To search Europe through, from Byzantium to Spain;
آ آ آ آ But neï؟½er shall you find, should you search till you tire,
آ آ آ آ So happy a man as the Barefooted Friar.

2.

آ آ آ آ Your knight for his lady pricks forth in career,
آ آ آ آ And is brought home at even-song prickï؟½d through with a spear;
آ آ آ آ I confess him in haste, for his lady desires
آ آ آ آ No comfort on earth save the Barefooted Friarï؟½s.

3.

آ آ آ آ Your monarch? Pshaw! many a prince has been known
آ آ آ آ To barter his robes for our cowl and our gown,
آ آ آ آ But which of us eï؟½er felt the idle desire
آ آ آ آ To exchange for a crown the grey hood of a Friar!

4.

آ آ آ آ The Friar has walkï؟½d out, and whereï؟½er he has gone,
آ آ آ آ The land and its fatness is markï؟½d for his own;
آ آ آ آ He can roam where he lists, he can stop when he tires,
آ آ آ آ For every manï؟½s house is the Barefooted Friarï؟½s.

5.

آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s expected at noon, and no wight till he comes
آ آ آ آ May profane the great chair, or the porridge of plums
آ آ آ آ For the best of the cheer, and the seat by the fire,
آ آ آ آ Is the undenied right of the Barefooted Friar.

6.

آ آ آ آ Heï؟½s expected at night, and the pastyï؟½s made hot,
آ آ آ آ They broach the brown ale, and they fill the black pot,
آ آ آ آ And the goodwife would wish the goodman in the mire,
آ آ آ آ Ere he lackï؟½d a soft pillow, the Barefooted Friar.

7.

آ آ آ آ Long flourish the sandal, the cord, and the cope,
آ آ آ آ The dread of the devil and trust of the Pope;
آ آ آ آ For to gather lifeï؟½s roses, unscathed by the briar,
آ آ آ آ Is granted alone to the Barefooted Friar.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter Scott (Sir) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Walter Scott (Sir)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
